ai_hardware.text: AI hardware is projected to grow at 22.5% CAGR (2025–2030), reaching $125B by 2030.
Analyst consensus for AI hardware is diverging — IQR widened from $22.6B to $111B over 12 years, signaling lower forecast confidence.
Scenario spread for AI hardware: Conservative $103B vs. Aggressive $169B by 2030 (64.3% delta), reflecting moderate uncertainty in growth assumptions.
AI hardware momentum decelerating: YoY growth decreased from 48.5% to -24.5% in 2025.
AI hardware ranks #3 across segments with 22.5% projected CAGR, 13.3% behind the leader (AI infrastructure at 35.8%).

ai_infrastructure.text: AI infrastructure is projected to grow at 35.8% CAGR (2025–2030), reaching $474B by 2030.
Analyst consensus for AI infrastructure is diverging — IQR widened from $51.1B to $252B over 12 years, signaling lower forecast confidence.
AI infrastructure leads all segments with 35.8% projected CAGR, 6.7% above the next fastest segment (AI software).
Scenario spread for AI infrastructure: Conservative $354B vs. Aggressive $744B by 2030 (110.0% delta), reflecting high uncertainty in growth assumptions.
AI infrastructure momentum decelerating: YoY growth decreased from 43.3% to 15.2% in 2025.

ai_software.text: AI software is projected to grow at 29.2% CAGR (2025–2030), reaching $258B by 2030.
Analyst consensus for AI software is diverging — IQR widened from $35.7B to $176B over 12 years, signaling lower forecast confidence.
Scenario spread for AI software: Conservative $202B vs. Aggressive $377B by 2030 (87.0% delta), reflecting moderate uncertainty in growth assumptions.
AI software momentum decelerating: YoY growth decreased from 16.6% to 9.8% in 2025.
AI software ranks #2 across segments with 29.2% projected CAGR, 6.7% behind the leader (AI infrastructure at 35.8%).

ai_adoption.text: AI adoption is projected to grow at 22.5% CAGR (2025–2030), reaching $61.5B by 2030.
Analyst consensus for AI adoption is diverging — IQR widened from $11.1B to $54.8B over 12 years, signaling lower forecast confidence.
Scenario spread for AI adoption: Conservative $50.8B vs. Aggressive $83.4B by 2030 (64.3% delta), reflecting moderate uncertainty in growth assumptions.
AI adoption momentum decelerating: YoY growth decreased from 32.3% to -9.7% in 2025.
AI adoption ranks #4 across segments with 22.5% projected CAGR, 13.3% behind the leader (AI infrastructure at 35.8%).
